title: Tiered Wooden Spice Rack
summary: Simple to follow step by step plans for a multi levelled spice rack
description: Simple to follow step by step plans for a multi levelled spice rack

steps.title: Cut the wood into the required pieces
steps.body:
Using the mitre saw, jig saw or table saw, cut the wood into the required sizes: 4 shelves, each : 12" x 2.5" (can also vary depending on your cabinet)

**Tip:** Cutting shelves to custom size Please remember that these shelf sizes can be altered to custom sizing. If changing the measurements, remember to alter all the following steps accordingly as well.

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes into shelves
steps.body: Using a KREG tools pocket hole jig, drill pocket holes into the ends of each shelf closer to one side.

steps.title: Cutting Risers
steps.body: Cut 3 pieces of wood into 1" x 12" pieces

steps.title: Cutting Side Pieces
steps.body: Cut out two 8" x 7" pieces of wood. Then mark out the 8" side 2.5" from the edge Then mark out the 7" side 3/4" from the edge Then cut diagonally between those two marks.

steps.title: Attaching Risers to shelf
steps.body: Using wood glue or super glue, attach a riser to each shelf on the same side as the pocket hole near the opposite edge of the pocket hole. Then attach each shelf to the next one to form the tiered shelves.

steps.title: Assemble Shelf
steps.body: Attach the two sides to the tiered shelving using pocket holes to securely fasten the piece together.

steps.title: Apply Wood finish of choice
steps.body:
Using a wood finish of preference, apply oil to tiered shelf and allow time to dry. I used a Tung Oil and resulted in a beautiful rich tone.

**Tip:** Follow the directions It's important to follow the directions on the label of whatever oil you decide to use.

steps.title: Finish Project with final details
steps.body: This is where you can get creative. I added these fancy spice bottles and placed the entire shelf into a cabinet.
